label: Deel
description: Manage global contractor payments, EOR employees, and compliance through Deel's unified workforce platform.

form.title: Connect Deel
form.subtitle: Paste your Deel API key to give Anton access to contractor, payroll, and EOR data.

methods.label: API Key
methods.description: Generate a personal API key from Deel's Developer settings and paste it here. No OAuth setup required — the fastest way to connect.
methods.how_to:
## Generate a Deel API Key

1. Log in to your Deel account at [app.deel.com](https://app.deel.com).
2. Click your avatar or account name in the bottom-left corner, then go to **Settings**.
3. In the left sidebar, navigate to **Developer → API**.
4. Click **Generate API Token** (or **Create new token** if tokens already exist).
5. Give the token a descriptive name like `Anton`.
6. Set the desired permission scopes — at minimum enable **Read** access for the resources Anton needs (contracts, payments, workers). Enable **Write** if you want Anton to take actions on your behalf.
7. Click **Generate**. The token is shown **once** — copy it immediately.
8. Paste the token into the **API Key** field below.

## Notes

- Tokens are scoped per workspace. If you manage multiple Deel organizations, generate a token for each.
- If you lose the token, delete it in Developer settings and generate a fresh one.
- Deel enforces IP-based rate limits; avoid running many parallel requests.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: API Key
fields.description: Found in Deel → Settings → Developer → API. The key is shown only once at creation time.

fields.name: base_url
fields.label: API Base URL
fields.description: Leave as default unless Deel has directed you to a regional endpoint.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (Client Credentials)
methods.description: Use a Deel OAuth app with a client ID and secret for server-to-server authentication. Suitable for organizations that need programmatic, non-user-scoped access.
methods.how_to:
## Connect Deel via OAuth 2.0

1. Log in to [app.deel.com](https://app.deel.com) and go to **Settings → Developer → OAuth Apps**.
2. Click **Create OAuth App**.
3. Enter a name (e.g. `Anton`) and set the redirect URI to the value Anton provides (displayed in the connector setup screen).
4. Select the required scopes: `contracts:read`, `payments:read`, `workers:read`, and `organizations:read`. Add write scopes if Anton needs to create or update records.
5. Save the app and copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** shown on the app detail page.
6. Paste both values into the fields below and click **Connect**. Anton will open Deel's consent page in your browser.
7. Approve the permissions. Deel redirects back to Anton and stores the resulting access and refresh tokens locally.

## Notes

- The client secret is shown once — save it before closing the page.
- If you need to re-authorize, delete the OAuth app in Deel and create a new one.
- OAuth tokens expire; Anton will automatically refresh them using the stored refresh token.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From Deel → Settings → Developer → OAuth Apps → your app's Client ID.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Shown once when the OAuth app is created. Paired with the Client ID above.
